items.title: investingLive European FX news wrap: Markets consolidate as US CPI looms
items.summary: US May NFIB small business optimism index 95.3 vs 96.0 expected USD/JPY holds above the 160.00 mark as traders look beyond the imminent BoJ rate hike Goldman Sachs no longer sees the Fed cutting interest rates this year Of cryptocurrencies and collectibles Indian Rupee maintains the bearish bias amid prolonged US-Iran stalemate, hawkish Fed risk What are the main events for today? German industrial production rebounds in April on higher energy, construction output FX option expiries for 9 June 10am New York cut BOJ reportedly set to raise short-term policy rate to 1% next week A quick US-Iran catchup ahead of European trading today US president Trump says he may have an "idea" on Iran deal within a few days Is the crypto market rebounding? Today's Ethereum analysis It's been a very boring session with no market-moving data or news releases. The only notable news was the Nikkei report confirming that the BoJ is going to hike interest rates to 1% next week and pause bond tapering from next fiscal year. There was little to no reaction in the JPY given that a rate hike was already priced in and the tapering pause removes the hawkish flavour of the rate hike. Early in the session, we got Trump touting "very good chances" of reaching a deal with Iran in the "next two or three days". CNN noted that Trump has claimed an Iran deal to be close at least 37 times already. I think it's unlikely to see a deal anytime soon after the latest escalation with Israel, so the stalemate might extend much further. In the markets, we've been seeing a positive mood with risk assets getting bid, the US dollar losing ground and oil prices extending losses following yesterday's Israel-Iran ceasefire. I think we're going to consolidate into the US CPI report tomorrow as that could either exacerbate or alleviate the hawkish Fed fears. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 美国5月NFIB小企业信心指数报95.3，低于预期值96.0；美元兑日元维持在160.00关口上方，交易员目光已转向日本央行即将到来的加息；高盛不再预期美联储今年降息；加密货币与收藏品市场；在美伊僵局持续及美联储鹰派风险的背景下，印度卢比维持看跌态势；今日有哪些主要事件？德国4月工业产出因能源及建筑业产出增长而反弹 6月9日纽约时间上午10点外汇期权到期 据报道日本央行下周将把短期政策利率上调至1% 今日欧洲交易时段前简要回顾美伊局势 美国总统特朗普表示，他可能在几天内就伊朗协议有“想法” 加密货币市场是否正在反弹？今日以太坊分析 今日交易时段相当沉闷，既无影响市场的数据发布，也无重大新闻。唯一值得关注的消息是日经新闻的报道，确认日本央行将于下周将利率上调至1%，并从下个财年起暂停缩减购债规模。鉴于加息已被市场充分消化，且暂停缩减购债消除了此次加息的鹰派色彩，日元汇率几乎未作反应。 开盘初期，特朗普宣称“未来两三天内”与伊朗达成协议的“机会非常大”。CNN指出，特朗普此前至少已37次声称与伊朗的协议近在咫尺。我认为在与以色列局势最新升级之后，短期内达成协议的可能性不大，因此僵局可能会持续更久。在市场方面，随着昨日以色列与伊朗达成停火协议，风险资产受到追捧，美元走软，油价延续跌势，整体市场情绪较为积极。我认为市场将在明日美国CPI报告发布前保持盘整，因为该报告可能加剧或缓解市场对美联储鹰派立场的担忧。本文作者为investinglive.com的Giuseppe Dellamotta。

German industrial production rebounds in April on higher energy, construction output FX option expiries for 9 June 10am New York cut BOJ reportedly set to raise short-term policy rate to 1% next week A quick US-Iran catchup ahead of European trading today US president Trump says he may have an "idea" on Iran deal within a few days Is the crypto market rebounding? Today's Ethereum analysis It's been a very boring session with no market-moving data or news releases. The only notable news was the Nikkei report confirming that the BoJ is going to hike interest rates to 1% next week and pause bond tapering from next fiscal year. There was little to no reaction in the JPY given that a rate hike was already priced in and the tapering pause removes the hawkish flavour of the rate hike. Early in the session, we got Trump touting "very good chances" of reaching a deal with Iran in the "next two or three days". I think we're going to consolidate into the US CPI report tomorrow as that could either exacerbate or alleviate the hawkish Fed fears. This article was written by Giuseppe Dellamotta at investinglive.com.
items.impactZh: 关键宏观数据超预期时，点差与滑点风险通常放大，建议降低仓位并控制杠杆。
items.impactEn: When macro data surprises, spread/slippage risk often widens; consider smaller position size and tighter leverage control.

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: OpenAI has confidentially filed for a US IPO
items.summary: Pentagon adds Alibaba, Baidu and BYD to China military-linked firms list China's May trade balance, exports and imports all higher than expected Daiwa sees BOJ June hike as bid to avoid falling behind the curve Australia business confidence improves in May but stays deeply negative China car sales fall 22% in May as oil prices crush petrol demand PBOC sets USD/ CNY reference rate for today at 6.8147 (vs. estimate at 6.7809) Buy USD/JPY dips on yen intervention, market forces point higher, RBC says Japan economy minister flags rate rise risks as BOJ decision nears ( South Korea halts programme trade with Kospi jumping more than 5% Preview: ECB to hike rates to 2.25% Thursday as oil inflation risk mounts. What's next? UK retail sales surge in May with biggest annual rise since April 2025 API oil chief warns US Strategic Petroleum Reserve nearing critical low US May CPI forecasts from 15 investment bank analysts. Hot, hot, hot ... FOMC sweating. Trump says going to "win it over the next two weeks". Usual lies and bluster? OpenAI files confidential IPO prospectus, suspected September market debut investingLive Americas FX news wrap 8 Jun: Dollar mixed as yields rise and stocks split BofA warns 70% of bear market signals now triggered for S&P 500 Iran says again no deal unless sanctions relief and release of frozen funds A mix day for the US stock indices. Dow closes lower. S&P and NASDAQ rise AstraZeneca obesity pill cuts body weight by 10.5% in phase II trial Summary: OpenAI has confidentially filed for a US IPO, targeting a valuation of up to $1 trillion with a potential September debut, joining Anthropic and SpaceX in racing toward public markets on the back of the AI boom Trump predicted "total victory" over Iran within two weeks, claiming Tehran is willing to concede everything and promising oil prices will "come tumbling down"; Iran has rejected US revisions to the draft deal, insisting on guaranteed sanctions relief and access to frozen assets The PBOC set its strongest CNY fixing since February 14, 2023 South Korea Q1 GDP beat on both measures: QoQ final 1.8% vs 1.7% expected and prior; YoY 3.8% vs 3.6% expected and prior China May trade data beat across the board: exports +19.4% y/y vs +15.0% expected, imports +27.4% vs +25.0% expected, trade surplus $105.43B vs $92.1B expected; oil imports hit an eight-year low Pentagon added Alibaba, Baidu, BYD and others to its 1260H China military-linked firms list, barring direct Defence Department contracts from later this month Washington asked Beijing to resume rare earth exports to Japan amid concerns over global high-tech supply chain disruption, per Nikkei Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Galuzin said Russia and Belarus are constantly ready to deploy all available means including nuclear weapons to ensure their security OpenAI became the latest AI heavyweight to take steps toward a public listing, confirming it has confidentially filed for a US IPO. The ChatGPT maker disclosed no size, terms or firm timeline, noting only that certain strategic objectives may be more easily pursued while it remains private. Reuters has reported the company is targeting a valuation of up to $1 trillion, with a September debut a possibility. The filing puts OpenAI alongside Anthropic and SpaceX in a cohort of high-profile private technology companies moving to capitalise on investor appetite for AI exposure. The dominant geopolitical theme remained the US-Iran conflict, though oil markets traded quietly in the absence of fresh escalation. President Trump struck an optimistic tone at a Republican tele-rally, predicting total victory within two weeks and claiming Iranian negotiators are prepared to concede on all key points, adding that oil prices would fall sharply once a deal is reached. Via a WaPo reporter: Tehran told a different story. Iranian officials rejected the latest US revisions to the draft agreement, reiterating through Al Jazeera that no ceasefire or deal is possible without guaranteed sanctions relief and the release of frozen assets, leaving the gap between the two sides very much open. The PBOC set its yuan reference rate at the strongest level since February 14, 2023, a notable signal of official support for the currency against the backdrop of ongoing trade and geopolitical pressures. On the data front, South Korea delivered a clean beat on final Q1 GDP, with quarterly growth confirmed at 1.8% against a 1.7% expectation and prior reading, and annual growth at 3.8% versus 3.6% expected. China's May trade figures also exceeded forecasts across all three headline measures, with exports up 19.4% year-on-year against a 15.0% consensus, imports rising 27.4% versus 25.0% expected, and the trade surplus coming in at $105.43 billion well above the $92.1 billion forecast. The import strength points to either genuine domestic demand recovery or front-loaded stockpiling, or a combination of both. China's oil imports in May fell to an eight-year low, a data point that will attract attention in energy markets. In a significant supply chain development, Washington asked Beijing to resume rare earth exports to Japan, with the Nikkei reporting US concern about the broader impact of restrictions on global high-technology supply chains, a sign that the rare earth issue is now being treated as a multilateral problem rather than purely a bilateral one. The Pentagon's addition of Alibaba, Baidu, BYD and others to its 1260H military-linked firms list added another layer of tension to the US-China relationship, even as diplomatic channels remain formally open following last month's Trump-Xi summit. Adding to the week's geopolitical backdrop, Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Mikhail Galuzin told Izvestia that Russia and Belarus remain constantly ready to use all available means, including nuclear weapons, to guarantee their security, language that will keep European risk sentiment on edge. Asia-Pacific equities traded with a mainly positive bias, recouping some of Monday's losses, though the overall tone was cautious. The US dollar held broadly steady, gold edged modestly higher, and oil traded in a narrow range with little fresh catalysts to drive direction. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 五角大楼将阿里巴巴、百度和比亚迪列入与中国军方有关联的企业名单 中国5月贸易收支、进出口数据均好于预期 大和证券认为日本央行6月加息旨在避免政策滞后 澳大利亚5月商业信心有所改善，但仍处于深度负值区间 受油价重创汽油需求影响，中国5月汽车销量下滑22% 中国人民银行将今日美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8147 （市场预期为6.7809）RBC称，日元干预时可逢低买入美元/日元，市场力量指向汇率走高 日本经济大臣在日央行决议临近之际警示加息风险（韩国暂停计划内贸易，Kospi指数跳涨逾5% 预览：随着石油通胀风险加剧，欧洲央行周四将加息至2.25%。接下来会怎样？英国5月零售额激增，创2025年4月以来最大年度涨幅。美国石油协会(API)负责人警告称，美国战略石油储备已接近危险低位。15位投行分析师对美国5月CPI的预测。火热、火热、火热……美联储公开市场委员会(FOMC)正焦头烂额。特朗普表示将在“未来两周内赢得胜利”。 又是老一套的谎言和虚张声势？OpenAI提交保密IPO招股书，预计9月上市 investingLive美洲外汇新闻综述 6月8日：收益率上升、股市涨跌不一，美元走势震荡 美银警告：标普500指数70%的熊市信号已触发 伊朗重申：除非解除制裁并解冻资金，否则不会达成协议 美国股指今日涨跌互现。道指收跌，标普500和纳斯达克指数上涨 阿斯利康减肥药在II期试验中使体重减少10.5% 摘要： OpenAI已秘密提交美国IPO申请，目标估值高达1万亿美元，或于9月上市，借AI热潮之势，与Anthropic和SpaceX一同竞相进军公开市场；特朗普预测将在两周内对伊朗取得“全面胜利”，声称德黑兰方面愿意做出一切让步，并承诺油价将“暴跌”； 伊朗拒绝了美国对协议草案的修订，坚持要求获得有保障的制裁解除及解冻资产的权利 中国人民银行将人民币中间价定为2023年2月14日以来的最高水平 韩国第一季度GDP双双超预期：环比终值为1.8%，高于预期值1.7%及前值；同比3.8%，高于预期值3.6%及前值 中国5月贸易数据全面超预期：预期 出口同比增长19.4%，高于预期值15.0%；进口同比增长27.4%，高于预期值25.0%；贸易顺差达1054.3亿美元，高于预期值921亿美元；原油进口量创八年来新低。五角大楼将阿里巴巴、百度、比亚迪等公司列入其1260H中国军方关联企业名单， 自本月下旬起禁止这些企业与美国国防部签订直接合同。据日经新闻报道，鉴于对全球高科技供应链中断的担忧，华盛顿方面已要求北京恢复对日本的稀土出口。俄罗斯副外长加卢津表示，俄罗斯和白俄罗斯随时准备动用包括核武器在内的所有可用手段来确保自身安全。OpenAI成为最新一家迈向公开上市的人工智能巨头，确认已秘密提交美国IPO申请。这家ChatGPT的开发商未披露募资规模、条款或确切时间表，仅指出在保持私有状态时，某些战略目标可能更容易实现。路透社报道称，该公司目标估值高达1万亿美元，可能于9月上市。此次申报使OpenAI与Anthropic和SpaceX一道，跻身于一批备受瞩目的私营科技公司之列，这些公司正试图利用投资者对人工智能投资机会的渴望来筹集资金。 尽管没有出现新的局势升级，油市交易依然平静，但美伊冲突仍是地缘政治的主旋律。特朗普总统在共和党的一次电话集会上表现得信心满满，预测将在两周内取得全面胜利，并声称伊朗谈判代表已准备在所有关键问题上做出让步，还补充说一旦达成协议，油价将大幅下跌。据《华盛顿邮报》记者报道：德黑兰方面则给出了截然不同的说法。伊朗官员拒绝了美国对协议草案的最新修订，并通过半岛电视台重申，若无法确保解除制裁并解冻资产，就不可能达成停火或协议，这使得双方的分歧依然巨大。中国人民银行将人民币中间价定在2023年2月14日以来的最高水平，在持续的贸易和地缘政治压力背景下，这是官方支持人民币汇率的显著信号。在数据方面，韩国第一季度GDP终值表现强劲，季度增长率确认达到1.8%，高于1.7%的预期和前值，年增长率为3.8%，高于预期的3.6%。 中国5月份的贸易数据在三大主要指标上均超出预期：出口同比增长19.4%，高于市场普遍预期的15.0%；进口增长27.4%，高于预期的25.0%；贸易顺差达1054.3亿美元，远超921亿美元的预测值。进口的强劲表现可能表明国内需求真正复苏，或是提前囤积，也可能是两者的结合。中国5月份的原油进口量降至八年来的最低水平，这一数据将在能源市场引起关注。在供应链领域的一项重大进展中，华盛顿方面要求北京恢复对日本的稀土出口。《日经新闻》报道称，美国方面担忧限制措施对全球高科技供应链的广泛影响，这表明稀土问题现已被视为多边问题，而非单纯的双边问题。尽管在上月特朗普与习近平的峰会后，外交渠道仍保持正式畅通，但五角大楼将阿里巴巴、百度、比亚迪等企业列入其1260H军方关联企业名单，给中美关系增添了新的紧张因素。 本周的地缘政治局势进一步加剧，俄罗斯副外长米哈伊尔·加卢津向《消息报》表示，俄罗斯和白俄罗斯始终准备好动用一切可用手段（包括核武器）来保障自身安全，此类言论将使欧洲市场的风险情绪持续处于紧张状态。亚太股市整体呈上涨态势，收复了周一的部分跌幅，但整体基调仍显谨慎。美元汇率基本持稳，黄金小幅走高，原油在窄幅区间内震荡，缺乏新的催化剂来推动市场方向。本文由investinglive.com的伊蒙·谢里丹撰写。

Adding to the week's geopolitical backdrop, Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Mikhail Galuzin told Izvestia that Russia and Belarus remain constantly ready to use all available means, including nuclear weapons, to guarantee their security, language that will keep European risk sentiment on edge. Asia-Pacific equities traded with a mainly positive bias, recouping some of Monday's losses, though the overall tone was cautious. The US dollar held broadly steady, gold edged modestly higher, and oil traded in a narrow range with little fresh catalysts to drive direction. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

items.title: investingLive Asia-Pacific FX news wrap: The day the war ceasefire really hit the fan.
items.summary: War news dominated, read from the bottom up for chronology: Iran strikes US bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain as retaliation widens across region Iran attacks US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain after American strikes target Hormuz defense CENTCOM declares Iran strikes complete after three waves targeting Hormuz defenses Oil has risen as the US has launched wave after wave after wave of attacks on Iran US launches third wave of Iran strikes as ballistic missiles fired from Isfahan Second wave of US attacks on Iran said to be underway Iran foreign minister Araqchi warns US to leave region, pledges response to strikes US hits Iran air defenses in warning shot as Trump says deal talks to continue More on this news: US strikes southern Iran after Apache helicopter downed over Hormuz Reports of multiple explosions near Bandar Abbas, on coast of Strait of Hormuz Other: China May CPI 1.2% y/y (expected 1.3%) and -0.1% m/m (expected -0.2%) PBOC sets USD/ CNY central rate at 6.8130 (vs. estimate at 6.7749) Japan data: May PPI 6.3% y/y (expected 5.5%) and 0.9% m/m (expected 0.5%) Oil: Private inventory survey shows a headline crude oil draw much greater than expected US stocks close mixed in a very volatile trading session Summary: US and Iran exchange direct military strikes overnight in the most serious escalation since the conflict began in February Iran downed a US Army Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz; Washington responded with three waves of precision strikes on Iranian air defense, radar and ground control sites Tehran struck back across three fronts, targeting US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan with missiles and drones; IRGC claims 21 targets hit including F-35 hangars at Al-Azraq Iran warns of a crushing and decisive response to any further US action; Washington insists peace talks remain on track Oil rose around 1% in Asian trade but the move was modest and has not extended; markets appear to be in a wait-and-see posture US crude inventories fell for an eighth straight week, down 9.12 million barrels per API survey data; official EIA figures due Wednesday Japan's wholesale inflation hit its highest since March 2023 in May; yen-based import prices up 25.5% year-on-year, fastest since November 2022 China's factory-gate prices rose to a near four-year high in May while consumer inflation stalled, pointing to margin compression across the industrial sector Regional equities retreated on geopolitical risk; USD rangebound against major currencies What began as a localised incident over the Strait of Hormuz has, within the space of a single night, become the most significant direct military exchange between the United States and Iran since the conflict began in February. The sequence matters. A US Army Apache helicopter was downed by an Iranian drone the previous day. Washington responded today with three waves of precision strikes targeting the air defense and radar architecture that underpins Iran's control of Hormuz. Tehran then went well beyond a symmetric response, launching missiles and drones simultaneously against US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan, claiming 21 targets hit including F-35 hangars and command infrastructure at Al-Azraq. The geography of Iran's retaliation is the defining development. This is no longer a Hormuz story. Iran has demonstrated both the willingness and the reach to strike US air power assets across the broader Middle East, and has explicitly warned that anything further from Washington will draw a crushing and decisive response. Against that backdrop, the US official line that tonight's strikes were a warning shot designed not to derail peace negotiations looks very difficult to sustain. Trump says a deal is still close. Iran's Foreign Minister and the IRGC are saying something entirely different. Hormuz remains closed. Oil is up. The morning will be telling. Oil's initial reaction was a modest rise of around 1% in early Asian trade, a number that looks strikingly small given the scale of what unfolded. That move has not been sustained or extended, suggesting markets are either waiting for damage assessments and official confirmation before committing, or are pricing in the possibility that diplomatic back-channels remain open despite the rhetoric. Either way, the muted price response feels like a pause rather than a verdict. Beneath the geopolitical noise, the fundamental picture in crude was already tightening before the first strike was launched. US crude inventories fell for an eighth consecutive week according to the latest API survey data, with stocks down 9.12 million barrels in the week ended June 5. Gasoline stocks also declined. Eight straight weeks of draws is not a rounding error,- it is a trend, and it points to a US supply picture that was tightening independently of anything happening in the Strait of Hormuz. The official EIA numbers are due Wednesday morning US time. If they confirm the API read, the market will be sitting on a major geopolitical escalation and a sustained domestic inventory drawdown simultaneously. That combination may yet produce the price move that tonight's military exchanges alone did not. The data backdrop reinforced the inflationary pressure building across the region. Japan's wholesale inflation posted its largest year-on-year increase since March 2023 in May, while the Bank of Japan noted that yen-based import prices surged 25.5% annually, the fastest pace since November 2022. The numbers reflect how thoroughly the Middle East conflict and Hormuz disruption have fed through into import costs for an economy as exposed to energy imports as Japan's. In China, the picture was split. Consumer inflation stalled unexpectedly in May even as factory-gate prices climbed to their highest in nearly four years, rising for a third consecutive month and exceeding market forecasts. The divergence between accelerating producer costs and subdued household prices is a classic margin compression signal, but the broader message is the same as Japan's, global commodity and energy costs are piling pressure onto manufacturers and filtering through to cost of living across the region. Beyond oil, regional equity markets gave ground as the scale of the overnight US-Iran exchange registered on sentiment. The risk-off tone was measured rather than panicked, with the USD remaining rangebound against major currencies, suggesting markets are still in a wait-and-see posture rather than moving to price a full regional war scenario. That posture may not survive Wednesday's session intact. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
items.summaryZh: 战争新闻占据头条，请从下往上阅读以了解时间顺序： 伊朗袭击约旦、科威特和巴林的美国基地，该地区报复行动不断扩大 美国打击霍尔木兹防空系统后，伊朗袭击驻巴林的美国第五舰队 中央司令部宣布对霍尔木兹防空系统的三轮打击行动结束 随着美国对伊朗发动一波又一波的袭击，油价持续上涨 伊斯法罕发射弹道导弹之际，美国对伊朗发动第三轮打击 据称美国对伊朗的第二轮袭击正在进行 伊朗外长阿拉奇警告美国撤离该地区， 并誓言将对袭击作出回应 特朗普表示将继续谈判，美军对伊朗防空系统发动警告性打击 更多相关新闻：阿帕奇直升机在霍尔木兹海峡上空被击落后，美军袭击伊朗南部 据报霍尔木兹海峡沿岸的阿巴斯港附近发生多起爆炸 其他： 中国5月CPI同比1.2%（预期1.3%），环比-0.1%（预期-0.2%） 中国人民银行将美元兑人民币中间价定为6.8130（较 （预估为6.7749）日本数据：5月生产者物价指数（PPI）同比上涨6.3%（预期5.5%），环比上涨0.9%（预期0.5%）原油：私人库存调查显示，原油总库存降幅远超预期；在剧烈震荡的交易日中，美国股指收盘涨跌不一。摘要： 美国与伊朗于昨晚互发动直接军事打击，这是自2月冲突爆发以来局势最严重的升级；伊朗在霍尔木兹海峡上空击落一架美军阿帕奇直升机； 华盛顿随即对伊朗防空、雷达及地面控制设施发动了三轮精准打击；德黑兰在三个战线展开反击，使用导弹和无人机袭击了美国在巴林、科威特和约旦的军事基地；伊朗革命卫队宣称已击中21个目标，包括阿兹拉克基地的F-35机库；伊朗警告称，若美方采取进一步行动，将予以毁灭性且决定性的反击； 华盛顿坚称和平谈判仍在正轨上。亚洲交易时段油价上涨约1%，但涨幅有限且未能延续；市场似乎持观望态度。美国石油协会（API）调查数据显示，美国原油库存连续第八周下降，减少912万桶；美国能源信息署（EIA）官方数据将于周三公布。日本5月批发通胀率升至2023年3月以来的最高水平；日元计价的进口 价格同比上涨25.5%，创2022年11月以来最快增速。5月中国出厂价格升至近四年高位，而消费者通胀则停滞不前，这表明工业部门利润率正面临压缩。受地缘政治风险影响，区域股市回落； 美元兑主要货币维持区间震荡 起初仅是霍尔木兹海峡的一起局部事件，却在一夜之间演变为自2月冲突爆发以来，美国与伊朗之间最重大的直接军事交锋。事件的时序至关重要。此前一天，一架美军阿帕奇直升机被伊朗无人机击落。华盛顿今日以三波精准打击作为回应，目标直指支撑伊朗控制霍尔木兹海峡的防空和雷达体系。随后，德黑兰采取的行动远超对等报复，同时向巴林、科威特和约旦境内的美军基地发射导弹和无人机，声称击中了21个目标，包括F-35机库和位于阿兹拉克的指挥基础设施。伊朗报复行动的地理范围是决定性的发展。这已不再是霍尔木兹海峡的故事。 伊朗已展现出打击中东地区美军空中力量资产的意愿和能力，并明确警告称，华盛顿若再有任何举动，必将招致毁灭性且果断的回应。在此背景下，美国官方关于“今晚的空袭只是一记警告，旨在不破坏和平谈判”的说法，似乎很难站得住脚。特朗普称协议仍近在咫尺，而伊朗外长和伊朗革命卫队所表达的立场却截然不同。霍尔木兹海峡依然封锁，油价持续上涨。今晨的走势将见分晓。油价在亚洲早盘的初步反应是小幅上涨约1%，鉴于事态发展的规模，这一涨幅显得格外微弱。这一走势未能持续或扩大，表明市场要么在等待损害评估和官方确认后再做决定，要么是在定价中计入了尽管言辞激烈但外交后门仍保持开放的可能性。无论哪种情况，这种温和的价格反应都更像是一次暂停，而非最终定论。在地缘政治噪音之下，早在首次打击发动之前，原油的基本面形势就已经趋紧。 根据美国石油协会（API）最新调查数据，美国原油库存连续第八周下降，截至6月5日当周库存减少912万桶。汽油库存同样出现下滑。连续八周的库存减少并非四舍五入的误差——这是一种趋势，表明美国供应形势正在趋紧，且这一趋势与霍尔木兹海峡的局势无关。美国能源信息署（EIA）的官方数据将于美国时间周三上午公布。若数据证实了API的读数，市场将同时面临重大地缘政治局势升级与国内库存持续减少的双重局面。这种组合或许会引发今晚仅靠军事交火未能引发的价格波动。这一数据背景加剧了该地区不断积聚的通胀压力。日本5月批发通胀率录得2023年3月以来最大同比增幅，日本央行同时指出，以日元计价的进口价格同比飙升25.5%，创2022年11月以来最快增速。这些数据反映出，对于像日本这样高度依赖能源进口的经济体而言，中东冲突和霍尔木兹海峡供应中断已对进口成本产生了多深远的影响。在中国，情况则呈现两极分化。 尽管出厂价格升至近四年来的最高水平，连续第三个月上涨且超出市场预期，但5月份的消费者通胀却意外停滞。生产者成本加速上涨与家庭消费价格涨幅温和之间的背离，是典型的利润率压缩信号，但其传递的更广泛信息与日本的情况如出一辙：全球大宗商品和能源成本正给制造商带来巨大压力，并逐渐传导至该地区的生活成本。除油价外，随着隔夜美伊交火的规模对市场情绪产生影响，区域股市也出现回落。市场风险规避情绪较为克制，并未陷入恐慌，美元兑主要货币汇率维持区间震荡，这表明市场仍持观望态度，而非开始定价全面区域战争的场景。这种态势在周三的交易时段中可能难以维持。本文作者为investinglive.com的Eamonn Sheridan。
items.summaryEn: War news dominated, read from the bottom up for chronology: Iran strikes US bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain as retaliation widens across region Iran attacks US Fifth Fleet in Bahrain after American strikes target Hormuz defense CENTCOM declares Iran strikes complete after three waves targeting Hormuz defenses Oil has risen as the US has launched wave after wave after wave of attacks on Iran US launches third wave of Iran strikes as ballistic missiles fired from Isfahan Second wave of US attacks on Iran said to be underway Iran foreign minister Araqchi warns US to leave region, pledges response to strikes US hits Iran air defenses in warning shot as Trump says deal talks to continue More on this news: US strikes southern Iran after Apache helicopter downed over Hormuz Reports of multiple explosions near Bandar Abbas, on coast of Strait of Hormuz Other: China May CPI 1.2% y/y (expected 1.3%) and -0.1% m/m (expected -0.2%) PBOC sets USD/ CNY central rate at 6.8130 (vs. estimate at 6.7749) Japan data: May PPI 6.3% y/y (expected 5.5%) and 0.9% m/m (expected 0.5%) Oil: Private inventory survey shows a headline crude oil draw much greater than expected US stocks close mixed in a very volatile trading session Summary: US and Iran exchange direct military strikes overnight in the most serious escalation since the conflict began in February Iran downed a US Army Apache helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz; Washington responded with three waves of precision strikes on Iranian air defense, radar and ground control sites Tehran struck back across three fronts, targeting US bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan with missiles and drones; IRGC claims 21 targets hit including F-35 hangars at Al-Azraq Iran warns of a crushing and decisive response to any further US action; Washington insists peace talks remain on track Oil rose around 1% in Asian trade but the move was modest and has not extended; markets appear to be in a wait-and-see posture US crude inventories fell for an eighth straight week, down 9.12 million barrels per API survey data; official EIA figures due Wednesday Japan's wholesale inflation hit its highest since March 2023 in May; yen-based import prices up 25.5% year-on-year, fastest since November 2022 China's factory-gate prices rose to a near four-year high in May while consumer inflation stalled, pointing to margin compression across the industrial sector Regional equities retreated on geopolitical risk; USD rangebound against major currencies What began as a localised incident over the Strait of Hormuz has, within the space of a single night, become the most significant direct military exchange between the United States and Iran since the conflict began in February. The sequence matters. A US Army Apache helicopter was downed by an Iranian drone the previous day. Washington responded today with three waves of precision strikes targeting the air defense and radar architecture that underpins Iran's control of Hormuz. Tehran then went well beyond a symmetric response, launching missiles and drones simultaneously against US military bases in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan, claiming 21 targets hit including F-35 hangars and command infrastructure at Al-Azraq. The geography of Iran's retaliation is the defining development. This is no longer a Hormuz story. Iran has demonstrated both the willingness and the reach to strike US air power assets across the broader Middle East, and has explicitly warned that anything further from Washington will draw a crushing and decisive response. Against that backdrop, the US official line that tonight's strikes were a warning shot designed not to derail peace negotiations looks very difficult to sustain. Trump says a deal is still close. Iran's Foreign Minister and the IRGC are saying something entirely different. Hormuz remains closed. Oil is up. The morning will be telling. Oil's initial reaction was a modest rise of around 1% in early Asian trade, a number that looks strikingly small given the scale of what unfolded. That move has not been sustained or extended, suggesting markets are either waiting for damage assessments and official confirmation before committing, or are pricing in the possibility that diplomatic back-channels remain open despite the rhetoric. Either way, the muted price response feels like a pause rather than a verdict. Beneath the geopolitical noise, the fundamental picture in crude was already tightening before the first strike was launched. US crude inventories fell for an eighth consecutive week according to the latest API survey data, with stocks down 9.12 million barrels in the week ended June 5. Gasoline stocks also declined. Eight straight weeks of draws is not a rounding error,- it is a trend, and it points to a US supply picture that was tightening independently of anything happening in the Strait of Hormuz. The official EIA numbers are due Wednesday morning US time. If they confirm the API read, the market will be sitting on a major geopolitical escalation and a sustained domestic inventory drawdown simultaneously. That combination may yet produce the price move that tonight's military exchanges alone did not. The data backdrop reinforced the inflationary pressure building across the region. Japan's wholesale inflation posted its largest year-on-year increase since March 2023 in May, while the Bank of Japan noted that yen-based import prices surged 25.5% annually, the fastest pace since November 2022. The numbers reflect how thoroughly the Middle East conflict and Hormuz disruption have fed through into import costs for an economy as exposed to energy imports as Japan's. In China, the picture was split. Consumer inflation stalled unexpectedly in May even as factory-gate prices climbed to their highest in nearly four years, rising for a third consecutive month and exceeding market forecasts. The divergence between accelerating producer costs and subdued household prices is a classic margin compression signal, but the broader message is the same as Japan's, global commodity and energy costs are piling pressure onto manufacturers and filtering through to cost of living across the region. Beyond oil, regional equity markets gave ground as the scale of the overnight US-Iran exchange registered on sentiment. The risk-off tone was measured rather than panicked, with the USD remaining rangebound against major currencies, suggesting markets are still in a wait-and-see posture rather than moving to price a full regional war scenario. That posture may not survive Wednesday's session intact. This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.
